name: cluster('{cluster_url}').database('{database_name}').table('JobSummary')
description: A summary of job execution details.

schema.description: A unique identifier for the job.
A hash value representing the job, used for quick comparison or deduplication.
The name assigned to the job, typically used for identification or display purposes.
The name of the user who submitted or owns the job.
The current state or status of the job, such as running, completed, failed, or queued.
The number of times the job has been retried after initial failure.
The unique identifier for a specific attempt of the job, incremented with each retry.
Detailed information about each retry attempt for the job, such as timestamps, error messages, and statuses.
The name of the virtual cluster where the job was executed or scheduled.
The total number of GPUs allocated to the job during its execution.
The priority level assigned to the job, which may affect scheduling and resource allocation.
The total duration of the job in hours.
The total number of GPU hours consumed by the job, calculated as the sum of GPU usage across all allocated GPUs and the entire job duration.
The total number of GPU hours during which the allocated GPUs were idle while the job was running.
The total number of GPU hours during which the allocated GPUs were effectively utilized for computation, excluding idle periods.
The timestamp indicating when the job was initially submitted to the system.
The timestamp indicating when the job started execution on the compute resources.
The timestamp indicating when the job completed execution on the compute resources.
The timestamp indicating when the job was created.
The percentage of time the allocated GPUs were idle while the job was running.
The percentage of time the allocated GPUs were actively utilized for computation.
The percentage of time the allocated GPUs were effectively utilized for computation, excluding idle periods.
The reason for the job's termination or completion.
The category of the job's exit status.
The timestamp indicating when the job was last updated.
The endpoint URL for accessing the job's results.
